source_test: HEC.FDA.Model/metrics/AssuranceResultStorage.cs (no upstream unit test file exists for this class; oracle-gate-reproduced against the real AssuranceResultStorage/PutDataIntoHistogram/AddObservation directly)
note: construct = {assurance_type, bin_width, convergence: {min_iterations, max_iterations}, standard_non_exceedance_probability}, matching the AssuranceResultStorage(string, double, ConvergenceCriteria, double) compute ctor; ConvergenceCriteria is built with the 2-arg (minIterations, maxIterations) ctor (ZAlpha/Tolerance left at their defaults), same convention as the aggregated_consequences_binned fixture. `observations` is a list of {iteration, result} applied in order via AddObservation(result, iteration) before a single PutDataIntoHistogram() call -- ONE object per case, shared across all of the case's assertions. Assertion `method` dispatches sample_mean (args []) or inverse_cdf (args [p]), both read off AssuranceHistogram. Cases: (1) single_iteration_narrow -- IterationCount pinned to 1 via the ConvergenceCriteria quirk (MaxIterations < 100 forces IterationCount = 1), so the one observation fills the entire staging array and the histogram is single-valued, exercising DynamicHistogram::inverse_cdf's histogram_is_single_valued short-circuit (returns SampleMean regardless of p); (2) multi_iteration_partial -- IterationCount defaults to 100 (MaxIterations >= 100), only 6 of the 100 staging slots are written by AddObservation, so the untouched 0.0 slots are included in the single PutDataIntoHistogram/AddObservationsToHistogram call alongside the real observations (unlike AggregatedConsequencesBinned, AssuranceHistogram's bin width is fixed at construction, not derived from the staged array's min/max, so this is ordinary eager-histogram behavior rather than the staged-array min/max quirk).
